name: The Barranc Salat canyon and the Mascarat coves
name_original: El cañón del Barranc Salat y las calas del Mascarat
region_intro: Hiking around Altea offers diverse terrain, from coastal paths along the Mediterranean to rugged mountain ranges. The region is characterized by imposing limestone ridges, such as the Sierra Helada Natural Park, and the more challenging Bernia mountain range. Hikers can explore a varied landscape that includes dramatic cliffside views, lush valleys, and accessible coastal routes. This combination provides a range of options for different hiking preferences. Best hiking trails around Altea The most popular hiking route is Albir Lighthouse – Albir Lighthouse loop from Altea, a 7.7 miles (12.3 km) moderate trail that takes 3 hours 25 minutes to complete. This route offers coastal views and passes by historical ochre mines. Another top favourite among local hikers is Ascent to Fort de Bèrnia (PR-CV 7), a difficult 11.5 miles (18.4 km) path. This trail leads through the Bernia mountain range, providing significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Local hikers also love the Circular walk in Altea, a 2.5 miles (4.0 km) trail leading through the town's surroundings, often completed in about 1 hour 4 minutes. Hiking around Altea is defined by limestone ridges, rugged mountains, and coastal paths. The network offers options for different ability levels, from easy strolls to challenging mountain ascents. The routes in Altea are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. More than 5900 hikers have used komoot to explore Altea's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available around Altea?
A: Altea offers a wide variety of hiking experiences, with over 110 trails to explore. These routes cater to all levels, from gentle coastal strolls to challenging mountain ascents.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Altea?
A: The Mediterranean climate makes Altea suitable for hiking most of the year. However, &lt;strong&gt;spring and autumn are particularly delightful&lt;/strong&gt;, offering pleasant temperatures and vibrant landscapes. Summers can be hot, so early morning or late afternoon hikes are recommended during those months.

Q: What are the general difficulty levels of hikes in Altea?
A: Altea's hiking network provides options for all abilities. You'll find approximately 47 easy trails, 50 moderate routes, and 14 more challenging hikes, ensuring there's something for every skill level.

Q: Are there family-friendly hiking trails in Altea?
A: Yes, Altea has several family-friendly options. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870546812/circular-walk-in-altea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk in Altea&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 4 km trail perfect for a leisurely stroll. Additionally, the Altea Ecological Walk and the Blue Trail of the Bay of Altea offer accessible coastal paths suitable for families, often with information panels on local flora and fauna.

Q: Can I find hikes with waterfalls near Altea?
A: While not directly in Altea, the &lt;strong&gt;Fonts de l'Algar (Algar Waterfalls)&lt;/strong&gt; are a popular nearby attraction for a day trip. You can explore hiking routes along the Algar River that wind through lush forests and offer opportunities to swim in crystal-clear waters. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/398248" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Algar Springs Waterfalls&lt;/a&gt; are a highlight in this area.

Q: What kind of natural features or landmarks can I expect to see on hikes in Altea?
A: Altea's hikes offer a diverse range of natural beauty and landmarks. You can expect dramatic cliffside views in the Sierra Helada Natural Park, rugged mountain scenery in the Bernia range, and stunning coastal panoramas. Notable features include the historical ochre mines along the path to Albir Lighthouse, the 16th-century Fort de Bernia, and the iconic &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4632066" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Bridge viewpoint&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4071030" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Mascarat Canyon&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there any circular walking routes in Altea?
A: Yes, Altea offers several circular walks. A popular easy option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e870546812/circular-walk-in-altea" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Circular walk in Altea&lt;/a&gt;, which takes you through the town's surroundings. For a moderate coastal experience, consider the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/38255645" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Albir Lighthouse – Albir Lighthouse loop from Altea&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: What do other hikers enjoy the most about hiking in Altea?
A: The hiking trails around Altea are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.4 stars from over 800 reviews. Hikers frequently praise the &lt;strong&gt;diverse landscapes&lt;/strong&gt;, from stunning coastal paths with sea views to challenging mountain trails, and the opportunity to discover both natural beauty and historical sites.

Q: Are there any challenging mountain hikes for experienced hikers?
A: Absolutely. For experienced hikers seeking a challenge, the Bernia mountain range offers rigorous trails. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e862998301/ascent-to-fort-de-bernia-pr-cv-7" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ascent to Fort de Bèrnia (PR-CV 7)&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 18.4 km path with significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Puig Campana, one of the highest peaks in the Valencia region, also presents a demanding full-day adventure with unparalleled views.

Q: Are there any coastal walks or trails with sea views in Altea?
A: Altea is renowned for its beautiful coastal paths offering stunning sea views. The Sierra Helada Natural Park features dramatic cliffside trails overlooking the Mediterranean. You can also enjoy the Altea Ecological Walk or the Blue Trail of the Bay of Altea, which provide accessible routes along the coast with picturesque vistas.

Q: Can I spot any unique wildlife or birds while hiking in Altea?
A: While hiking in Altea, particularly in areas like the Sierra Helada Natural Park, you might encounter various bird species, including seabirds. The nearby &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/4371964" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;El Saladar Saltworks&lt;/a&gt; and &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/889730" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Viewpoint of the Salinas de Calpe&lt;/a&gt; are known for their birdlife, including flamingos, offering unique wildlife viewing opportunities.

Q: Are there any hikes that offer historical insights?
A: Yes, several trails in Altea combine natural beauty with historical significance. The route to the Albir Lighthouse passes by historical remnants of ochre mines. In the Bernia mountain range, trails lead past the 16th-century Fort de Bernia, offering a glimpse into the region's past.
tips: An amazing place with incredible views to enjoy the sea and the coves of Mascarat. It is easily accessed from the town and allows you to explore the coves on each side. A wonderful place to enjoy the sunrise over the Mediterranean Sea

Between the Alicante towns of Altea and Calpe, the N-332 road crosses a rugged and rugged landscape, as spectacular as few. The great natural barrier of the Serra de Bèrnia, which sinks its roots into the sea in the Morro de Toix, is broken by the Barranc Salat, which brings together the waters collected by the Bèrnia itself and the Sierra de Oltà, flowing into the sea in the nearby Playa del Mascarat (being the origin of it).
